RNA-ADT Mutual Information With GSE164378 3’ CITE-seq

This tutorial uses the paired GSE164378 3’ CITE-seq example to estimate RNA-to-ADT spectral mutual information. RNA is the measured modality X; ADT is the paired target modality Y.

Fit RNA and ADT

r <- 12

rna_fit <- scscale_fit(
  rna_counts,
  r = r,
  fit_umi = FALSE,
  mp_max_iter = 20,
  mp_grid_n = 4000,
  store_matrix = TRUE
)

adt_fit <- scscale_fit(
  adt_counts,
  r = r,
  fit_umi = FALSE,
  mp_max_iter = 20,
  mp_grid_n = 4000
)

data.frame(
  modality = c("RNA", "ADT"),
  p = c(rna_fit$p, adt_fit$p),
  n = c(rna_fit$n, adt_fit$n),
  c_X = c(rna_fit$c_X, adt_fit$c_X),
  tau2 = c(rna_fit$bulk$tau2, adt_fit$bulk$tau2),
  lambda_plus = c(rna_fit$bulk$lambda_plus, adt_fit$bulk$lambda_plus),
  selected_iteration = c(rna_fit$bulk$selected_iteration, adt_fit$bulk$selected_iteration)
)
##   modality    p    n   c_X      tau2 lambda_plus selected_iteration
## 1      RNA 2000 4000 0.500 0.6017899   1.7537442                  9
## 2      ADT  228 4000 0.057 0.1435733   0.2203123                 17
theta_Y <- adt_fit$theta_X[seq_len(r)]

data.frame(
  I_theory = scscale_theory_mi(rna_fit, adt_fit, r = r)$I_theory,
  I_infinity = scscale_i_infinity(rna_fit, adt_fit, r = r)$I_infinity
)
##   I_theory I_infinity
## 1 16.76347   16.85009

UMI Scaling Law

sampling_rates <- c(0.10, 0.20, 0.35, 0.50, 0.70, 0.85, 1.00)

umi_scaling <- scscale_umi_scaling(
  rna_counts,
  sampling_rates = sampling_rates,
  r = r,
  seed = 164378,
  n_replicates = 1,
  theta_Y = theta_Y,
  reference_fit = rna_fit,
  empirical = FALSE,
  mp_max_iter = 20,
  mp_grid_n = 2500
)

umi_mi <- unique(umi_scaling$scaling[, c(
  "sampling_rate", "U", "total_umi_observed", "I_theory", "I_infinity"
)])

umi_mi
##   sampling_rate        U total_umi_observed  I_theory I_infinity
## 1          0.10  2660343            2660797  7.747259    8.77668
## 7          0.20  5320685            5322255  9.793249   10.46800
## 6          0.35  9311199            9308479 11.394209   11.85884
## 3          0.50 13301713           13299605 12.450799   12.80530
## 2          0.70 18622398           18622917 13.430307   13.69801
## 5          0.85 22612912           22611795 13.997932   14.22210
## 4          1.00 26603426           26603426 14.478201   14.66962
umi_scaling$q_fit_rate
##    rank q_rate_intercept q_rate_slope q_rate_r2
## 1     1       32.4617781   186.313399 0.9915879
## 2     2        4.1912246    55.650972 0.9991288
## 3     3       11.9460550    25.094679 0.9815932
## 4     4        1.8405268    19.252367 0.9986517
## 5     5        3.3362273    15.794140 0.9962295
## 6     6        3.2309825     9.947315 0.9950053
## 7     7        1.8069190     9.499317 0.9911642
## 8     8        1.8214184     7.956526 0.9938037
## 9     9        0.6813628     5.855743 0.9978532
## 10   10        0.6709745     4.945185 0.9981538
## 11   11        0.8153936     4.238776 0.9986146
## 12   12        0.8256311     3.312713 0.9997054

Cell Scaling Curves

For a fixed sequencing rate rho, the fitted RNA spike strengths give d2_X(rho) = q_X(rho) / c_ref, where c_ref = p / n_ref at 4,000 cells. Changing cell number changes c_X = p / n, while the fixed-UMI spike strengths are held on that sequencing-rate curve.

n_grid <- c(500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 4000)
rho_grid <- c(0.35, 0.50, 0.70, 0.85, 1.00)
c_ref <- rna_fit$c_X

cell_theta <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rho_grid, function(rho) {
  q_df <- unique(umi_scaling$scaling[umi_scaling$scaling$sampling_rate == rho,
                                    c("rank", "q_X_rate_hat")])
  q_df <- q_df[order(q_df$rank), ]
  d2_rho <- q_df$q_X_rate_hat / c_ref
  out <- scscale_cell_scaling(d2_rho, p = rna_fit$p, n_grid = n_grid, theta_Y = theta_Y)
  transform(out, sampling_rate = rho)
}))

cell_curves <- aggregate(
  I_theory ~ n + c_X + sampling_rate,
  data = cell_theta,
  FUN = unique
)

cell_curves
##       n       c_X sampling_rate I_theory
## 1  4000 0.5000000          0.35 11.24307
## 2  3000 0.6666667          0.35 12.39179
## 3  2000 1.0000000          0.35 13.95909
## 4  1000 2.0000000          0.35 16.34633
## 5   500 4.0000000          0.35 18.19337
## 6  4000 0.5000000          0.50 12.32204
## 7  3000 0.6666667          0.50 13.44440
## 8  2000 1.0000000          0.50 14.94151
## 9  1000 2.0000000          0.50 17.13686
## 10  500 4.0000000          0.50 18.75222
## 11 4000 0.5000000          0.70 13.39019
## 12 3000 0.6666667          0.70 14.46756
## 13 2000 1.0000000          0.70 15.87018
## 14 1000 2.0000000          0.70 17.84660
## 15  500 4.0000000          0.70 19.22929
## 16 4000 0.5000000          0.85 14.01446
## 17 3000 0.6666667          0.85 15.05614
## 18 2000 1.0000000          0.85 16.39187
## 19 1000 2.0000000          0.85 18.22880
## 20  500 4.0000000          0.85 19.47642
## 21 4000 0.5000000          1.00 14.53468
## 22 3000 0.6666667          1.00 15.54102
## 23 2000 1.0000000          1.00 16.81440
## 24 1000 2.0000000          1.00 18.52946
## 25  500 4.0000000          1.00 19.66599

Recoverability Check

theta_full <- cell_theta[cell_theta$sampling_rate == 1 & cell_theta$rank <= 6, ]
theta_cols <- c("#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#009E73", "#CC79A7", "#56B4E9", "#E69F00")

plot(
  NA,
  xlim = range(theta_full$n),
  ylim = range(theta_full$theta_X),
  xlab = "Cell number n",
  ylab = expression(theta[X*k](n)),
  main = expression("Recoverability at " * rho == 1)
)
for (k in sort(unique(theta_full$rank))) {
  df <- theta_full[theta_full$rank == k, ]
  df <- df[order(df$n), ]
  lines(df$n, df$theta_X, col = theta_cols[k], lwd = 2)
  points(df$n, df$theta_X, col = theta_cols[k], pch = 16)
}
legend(
  "bottomleft",
  legend = paste0("k=", 1:6),
  col = theta_cols,
  lwd = 2,
  pch = 16,
  bty = "n",
  cex = 0.85
)

Empirical d2 Check

If the population spike strength d_X^2 is stable, the estimates below should be roughly flat across sampled cell numbers at a fixed sequencing rate.

d2_grid <- expand.grid(
  sampling_rate = rho_grid,
  n = n_grid,
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE
)

u_ref <- svd(rna_fit$matrix, nu = r, nv = 0)$u[, seq_len(r), drop = FALSE]
d2_rows <- vector("list", nrow(d2_grid))
for (i in seq_len(nrow(d2_grid))) {
  rho <- d2_grid$sampling_rate[i]
  n_i <- d2_grid$n[i]
  set.seed(364378 + i)
  cells <- sample(colnames(rna_counts), n_i)

  rna_sub <- rna_counts[, cells, drop = FALSE]
  if (rho < 1) {
    rna_sub <- scscale_downsample_counts_fraction(rna_sub, fraction = rho, seed = 464378 + i)
  }

  X_sub <- scscale_normalize_counts(rna_sub, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)
  fit_sub <- scscale_fit(
    X_sub,
    input = "normalized",
    center = FALSE,
    scale = FALSE,
    r = r,
    mp_max_iter = 20,
    mp_grid_n = 1500
  )

  projected <- crossprod(u_ref, X_sub)
  v <- rowSums(projected^2) / ncol(X_sub)
  d2_hat <- pmax(v - fit_sub$bulk$tau2, 0) / fit_sub$bulk$tau2
  d2_rows[[i]] <- data.frame(
    sampling_rate = rho,
    n = n_i,
    rank = seq_len(r),
    tau2 = fit_sub$bulk$tau2,
    d2_X = d2_hat
  )
}

d2_empirical <- do.call(rbind, d2_rows)
d2_empirical[d2_empirical$rank <= 6, ]
##     sampling_rate    n rank         tau2      d2_X
## 1            0.35  500    1 8.287873e-01 193.44273
## 2            0.35  500    2 8.287873e-01  46.16098
## 3            0.35  500    3 8.287873e-01  37.75113

## 290          1.00 4000    2 6.025276e-01 118.50487
## 291          1.00 4000    3 6.025276e-01  72.08227
## 292          1.00 4000    4 6.025276e-01  41.70449
## 293          1.00 4000    5 6.025276e-01  37.66706
## 294          1.00 4000    6 6.025276e-01  25.97809
d2_ranks <- 1:6
d2_plot <- d2_empirical[d2_empirical$rank %in% d2_ranks, ]

old_par <- par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.4, 1))
for (rho in c(0.35, 0.70, 1.00)) {
  df_rho <- d2_plot[d2_plot$sampling_rate == rho, ]
  plot(
    NA,
    xlim = range(df_rho$n),
    ylim = range(df_rho$d2_X),
    xlab = "Sampled cell number n",
    ylab = expression(hat(d)[X*k]^2),
    main = bquote(rho == .(rho))
  )
  for (k in d2_ranks) {
    df <- df_rho[df_rho$rank == k, ]
    df <- df[order(df$n), ]
    lines(df$n, df$d2_X, col = theta_cols[k], lwd = 2)
    points(df$n, df$d2_X, col = theta_cols[k], pch = 16)
  }
}

par(old_par)

Empirical Comparison

The empirical points recompute RNA and ADT cell subspaces on matched cell subsamples. RNA is also globally thinned at the specified sequencing rate.

empirical_grid <- expand.grid(
  sampling_rate = rho_grid,
  n = n_grid,
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE
)

empirical_rows <- vector("list", nrow(empirical_grid))
for (i in seq_len(nrow(empirical_grid))) {
  rho <- empirical_grid$sampling_rate[i]
  n_i <- empirical_grid$n[i]
  set.seed(164378 + i)
  cells <- sample(colnames(rna_counts), n_i)

  rna_sub <- rna_counts[, cells, drop = FALSE]
  adt_sub <- adt_counts[, cells, drop = FALSE]
  if (rho < 1) {
    rna_sub <- scscale_downsample_counts_fraction(rna_sub, fraction = rho, seed = 264378 + i)
  }

  X <- scscale_normalize_counts(rna_sub, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)
  Y <- scscale_normalize_counts(adt_sub, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)
  z_X <- right_singular_vectors(X, r = r)
  z_Y <- right_singular_vectors(Y, r = r)
  mi_emp <- subspace_overlap_mi(z_X, z_Y)

  theory_match <- cell_curves[
    cell_curves$sampling_rate == rho & cell_curves$n == n_i,
    "I_theory"
  ]
  empirical_rows[[i]] <- data.frame(
    sampling_rate = rho,
    n = n_i,
    I_empirical = mi_emp$mi,
    I_theory = theory_match,
    r_eff = mi_emp$r_eff
  )
}

empirical_mi <- do.call(rbind, empirical_rows)
empirical_mi
##    sampling_rate    n I_empirical I_theory r_eff
## 1           0.35  500    4.670037 18.19337    12
## 2           0.50  500    5.078014 18.75222    12
## 3           0.70  500    5.298505 19.22929    12

## 14          0.85 2000    5.650348 16.39187    12
## 15          1.00 2000    5.650568 16.81440    12

## 18          0.70 3000    5.707376 14.46756    12
## 19          0.85 3000    5.689292 15.05614    12
## 20          1.00 3000    5.734177 15.54102    12
## 21          0.35 4000    5.349942 11.24307    12
## 22          0.50 4000    5.521447 12.32204    12
## 23          0.70 4000    5.643318 13.39019    12
## 24          0.85 4000    5.705811 14.01446    12
## 25          1.00 4000    5.740955 14.53468    12

Plots

cols <- c("0.35" = "#0072B2", "0.50" = "#D55E00", "0.70" = "#009E73",
          "0.85" = "#CC79A7", "1" = "#E69F00")

old_par <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4.2, 4.2, 2.4, 1))

plot(
  umi_mi$sampling_rate,
  umi_mi$I_theory,
  pch = 16,
  col = "#0072B2",
  type = "b",
  xlab = expression("Sampling rate " * rho),
  ylab = expression(I(rho)),
  main = "UMI scaling law"
)
lines(umi_mi$sampling_rate, umi_mi$I_infinity, col = "#D55E00", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("finite n", "infinite n"),
  col = c("#0072B2", "#D55E00"),
  pch = c(16, NA),
  lwd = c(1, 2),
  lty = c(1, 2),
  bty = "n"
)

plot(
  NA,
  xlim = range(n_grid),
  ylim = range(c(cell_curves$I_theory, empirical_mi$I_empirical)),
  xlab = "Cell number n",
  ylab = expression(I(n, rho)),
  main = "I(n, U) at fixed UMI rates"
)
for (rho in rho_grid) {
  df <- cell_curves[cell_curves$sampling_rate == rho, ]
  df <- df[order(df$n), ]
  lines(df$n, df$I_theory, col = cols[as.character(rho)], lwd = 2)
  emp <- empirical_mi[empirical_mi$sampling_rate == rho, ]
  points(emp$n, emp$I_empirical, pch = 16, col = cols[as.character(rho)])
}
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = paste0("rho=", rho_grid),
  col = cols[as.character(rho_grid)],
  lwd = 2,
  pch = 16,
  bty = "n",
  cex = 0.85
)

plot(
  n_grid,
  cell_curves[cell_curves$sampling_rate == 1, "I_theory"],
  type = "b",
  pch = 16,
  col = "#0072B2",
  xlab = "Cell number n",
  ylab = expression(I(n, rho == 1)),
  main = "Cell number scaling law"
)
emp_full <- empirical_mi[empirical_mi$sampling_rate == 1, ]
points(emp_full$n, emp_full$I_empirical, pch = 17, col = "#D55E00")
legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c("theory", "empirical"),
  col = c("#0072B2", "#D55E00"),
  pch = c(16, 17),
  lwd = c(1, NA),
  bty = "n"
)

plot(
  empirical_mi$I_theory,
  empirical_mi$I_empirical,
  pch = 16,
  col = cols[as.character(empirical_mi$sampling_rate)],
  xlab = "Theory",
  ylab = "Empirical",
  main = "Empirical comparison"
)
abline(0, 1, col = "black", lwd = 2, lty = 2)

par(old_par)
